{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|加毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年11月}} {{神社 |名称=加毛神社 |画像=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|加毛神社（輪之内町）]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}下大榑東井堰13017 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}} |例祭=9月30日 |創建=不明 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=加毛神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''加毛神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}安八郡加毛神社であり、全国にある{{jalink|加茂神社|Q11398885}}（賀茂神社・鴨神社）の一つとされている。 === 沿革・概略 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}（{{jalink|彦坐王|Q11487910}}の子。{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}・{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}の祖）、またはその子孫がこの地を開墾した際に、祖神の神別雷命を祀った神社と言われている[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）]。 創建当初の名は加毛神社（美濃国神名帳には「従五位下加毛明神」）であったが、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の頃から{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}を祀る神社となり、白髭神社と改名している。このことより、現在も一部の文献には祭神を「白髭大明神」としている。 {{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}（明治2年）に加毛神社に改称し、{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=加毛神社|format= |publisher=輪之内町商工会|language=日本語|accessdate=2025-11-14}}。 {{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）に岐阜県神社庁より{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される[https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社]。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} : {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}の説もある === 主な祭事 === * 元始祭 - 1月3日 * {{jalink|左義長|Q9332740}} - 1月第二日曜日 * 粥つけ神事 - 左義長の翌日 * 祈年祭 - 3月第一日曜日 * 例祭 - 9月30日 * 氏子感謝祭（加毛神社祭） - 10月第二日曜日 === 所在地 === * 岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017 : {{jalink|大榑川|Q11436448}}（{{jalink|長良川|Q1194094}}の支流）の右岸の{{jalink|福束輪中}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしやあんはちくん}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]]

name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年11月}} {{神社 |名称=加毛神社 |画像=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|加毛神社（輪之内町）]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}下大榑東井堰13017 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}} |例祭=9月30日 |創建=不明 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=加毛神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''加毛神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}安八郡加毛神社であり、全国にある{{jalink|加茂神社|Q11398885}}（賀茂神社・鴨神社）の一つとされている。 === 沿革・概略 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}（{{jalink|彦坐王|Q11487910}}の子。{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}・{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}の祖）、またはその子孫がこの地を開墾した際に、祖神の神別雷命を祀った神社と言われている[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）]。 創建当初の名は加毛神社（美濃国神名帳には「従五位下加毛明神」）であったが、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の頃から{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}を祀る神社となり、白髭神社と改名している。このことより、現在も一部の文献には祭神を「白髭大明神」としている。 {{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}（明治2年）に加毛神社に改称し、{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=加毛神社|format= |publisher=輪之内町商工会|language=日本語|accessdate=2025-11-14}}。 {{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）に岐阜県神社庁より{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される[https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社]。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} : {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}の説もある === 主な祭事 === * 元始祭 - 1月3日 * {{jalink|左義長|Q9332740}} - 1月第二日曜日 * 粥つけ神事 - 左義長の翌日 * 祈年祭 - 3月第一日曜日 * 例祭 - 9月30日 * 氏子感謝祭（加毛神社祭） - 10月第二日曜日 === 所在地 === * 岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017 : {{jalink|大榑川|Q11436448}}（{{jalink|長良川|Q1194094}}の支流）の右岸の{{jalink|福束輪中}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしやあんはちくん}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]]

link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年11月}} {{神社 |名称=加毛神社 |画像=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|加毛神社（輪之内町）]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}下大榑東井堰13017 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}} |例祭=9月30日 |創建=不明 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=加毛神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''加毛神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|輪之内町|Q1204291}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}安八郡加毛神社であり、全国にある{{jalink|加茂神社|Q11398885}}（賀茂神社・鴨神社）の一つとされている。 === 沿革・概略 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}（{{jalink|彦坐王|Q11487910}}の子。{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}・{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}の祖）、またはその子孫がこの地を開墾した際に、祖神の神別雷命を祀った神社と言われている[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）]。 創建当初の名は加毛神社（美濃国神名帳には「従五位下加毛明神」）であったが、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の頃から{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}を祀る神社となり、白髭神社と改名している。このことより、現在も一部の文献には祭神を「白髭大明神」としている。 {{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}（明治2年）に加毛神社に改称し、{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=加毛神社|format= |publisher=輪之内町商工会|language=日本語|accessdate=2025-11-14}}。 {{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）に岐阜県神社庁より{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される[https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社]。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|神別雷命}} : {{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|白髭大明神}}の説もある === 主な祭事 === * 元始祭 - 1月3日 * {{jalink|左義長|Q9332740}} - 1月第二日曜日 * 粥つけ神事 - 左義長の翌日 * 祈年祭 - 3月第一日曜日 * 例祭 - 9月30日 * 氏子感謝祭（加毛神社祭） - 10月第二日曜日 === 所在地 === * 岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017 : {{jalink|大榑川|Q11436448}}（{{jalink|長良川|Q1194094}}の支流）の右岸の{{jalink|福束輪中}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/　加毛神社] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85　加毛神社（岐阜県神社庁）] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしやあんはちくん}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Wanouchi Town|Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{jalink|Kamo Shrines|Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{jalink|Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336}} (son of {{jalink|Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} and {{jalink|Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{jalink|Sagichō|Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{jalink|Ōgure River|Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{jalink|Nagara River|Q1194094}}), in the {{jalink|Fukutsuka Wajū}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094}}), in the {{ill||lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo Shrine, Anpachi District}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} (Q11398661)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:輪之内町の建築物]] [[Category:輪之内町の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社|あんはちくん]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} ==
