== name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|WD=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|WD=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|WD=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|WD=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|WD=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|qid=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|qid=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|qid=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|qid=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|qid=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|qid=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|qid=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|qid=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|qid=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|qid=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|qid=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|qid=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|qid=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|qid=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|qid=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|qid=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{Shinto-stub}} == == name in kana (P1814) == * かもじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005004798 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡輪之内町下大榑東井堰13017', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:加毛神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398661}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name=Kamo Shrine |Image=[[File:Wanouchi kamo shrine.jpg|250px|Kamo Shrine (Wanouchi Town)]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}} 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise |Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Annual festival=September 30 |Founded=Unknown |Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|qid=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Wanouchi Town|Q1204291|qid=Q1204291|1=Wanouchi, Gifu}}. It is the Kamo Shrine of Anpachi District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is considered one of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrines|Q11398885|qid=Q11398885|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)}} (Kamo Shrine / Kamo Shrine) found throughout Japan. === History and Overview === The date of founding is unknown. It is said that {{ill|lt=Kamone no Ōkimi|Q119380336|qid=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}} (son of {{ill|lt=Hikozawano Ōkimi|Q11487910|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}; ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|qid=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and {{ill|lt=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|qid=Q11358204|1=Mino no Saki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}) or his descendants enshrined their ancestral deity, Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto, when they cultivated this land.[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Kamo Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. The original name at the time of founding was Kamo Shrine (in the Mino Province Jinmyōchō it appears as “Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade Kamo Myōjin”), but from around the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} it became a shrine enshrining {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} and was renamed Shirahige Shrine. For this reason, some documents still list the enshrined deity as “Shirahige Daimyōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|qid=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed Kamo Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://kanko.washoko.or.jp/detail.php?id=24|title=Kamo Shrine|publisher=Wanouchi Town Chamber of Commerce and Industry|accessdate=2025-11-14}} In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.[https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Kambe Wakareikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} : There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Major Festivals === * Ganshi-sai – January 3 * {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|qid=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – Second Sunday of January * Kayutsuke Shinji – The day after Sagichō * Kinen-sai – First Sunday of March * Annual Festival – September 30 * Parishioners’ Thanksgiving Festival (Kamo Shrine Festival) – Second Sunday of October === Location === * 13017 Shimōgure Higashi-ise, Wanouchi Town, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture : On the right bank of the {{ill|lt=Ōgure River|Q11436448|qid=Q11436448|1=Ōgure River}} (a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Nagara River|Q1194094|qid=Q1194094|1=Nagara River}}), in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukutsuka Wajū|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://kamo-shrine.jp/ Kamo Shrine] *
